definition: Copper has the strongest individual case for structural supply tightness among all commodities, driven by declining ore grades (~0.8% in 2005 to ~0.5% currently), depleted project pipeline, greenfield timelines of 12-18 years, and demand confluence from EVs, grid infrastructure, and data centers. However, deficit projections are systematically overestimated.
evidence: Six agents identify copper as the critical commodity
Demand confluence: EVs (~10M units 2025), grid (+15-20% YoY), renewables (300+ GW/yr)
Copper is the only base metal where Chinese demand intensity shows no structural break
0/8 major deficit forecasts fully materialized at projected magnitude (20-30% hit rate)
Zombie zone: prices above AISC ($8-9k/t) but below greenfield incentive ($10-11k/t)
open_questions: Will aluminum substitution prove material above $12,000/t copper?
Can brownfield expansion and scrap recovery close 30-50% of projected deficits?
Is the Kamoa-Kakula/Quellaveco/Oyu Tolgoi ramp sufficient to moderate deficit timing?
